data.content:
The build confirms the issue. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but **never adds it to the `Compile` item group**. SDK implicit globs are evaluated before targets run, so the generated file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `Compile` and `FileWrites` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add to Compile so the compiler sees it, and FileWrites so Clean removes it -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit globbing happens at evaluation time (before targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up automatically.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, after the file is generated. I should also add it to `<FileWrites>` so it gets cleaned properly.

This is exactly the scenario described in the "including-generated-files" skill - generated files not being compiled because globs expand at evaluation time before execution creates them.


Now I'll update the .csproj to include an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target that registers the generated file with the `Compile` items.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add to Compile so the compiler sees it, and FileWrites so Clean removes it -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Diagnosis:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file during build execution, but SDK implicit globs (`<Compile Include="**/*.cs">`) are expanded at *evaluation* time — before any targets run. So the generated file existed on disk but wasn't in the compilation.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target to explicitly include the generated file in `Compile` (so the compiler sees it) and `FileWrites` (so `dotnet clean` removes it).
